items.title: Gotterup beats Matsuyama in Phoenix Open play-off with Scheffler tied third
items.summary: Chris Gotterup came up on the rails to win the Phoenix Open after beating Hideki Matsuyama in a play-off.

items.title: VAR review: Liverpool anger justified in no red card for Man City?
items.summary: After Liverpool's loss, coach Arne Slot fumed that Man City deserved a red card: Is he right?

items.title: Man City's comeback thriller keeps Premier League title hopes alive; Liverpool slip further
items.summary: Liverpool looked set to win, but Man City's two unanswered goals mean the Premier League title race stays alive and Liverpool's Champions League hopes are in doubt.

items.title: Haaland earns City dramatic late win at Liverpool
items.summary: Erling Haaland kept Manchester City in the title race with an added-time penalty as Pep Guardiola's side came from behind to win 2-1 in a chaotic finish.

items.title: Pep warns Arsenal: 'A lot can happen' in title race
items.summary: Pep Guardiola has warned Arsenal that 'a lot can happen in 13 games' as the Premier League title race heats up.

items.title: Arbeloa: Mbappé can match Ronaldo at Madrid
items.summary: Coach Álvaro Arbeloa said Kylian Mbappé "has the qualities" to match the "out of this world" Cristiano Ronaldo's impact at Real Madrid.

items.title: Samenvatting: invaller Fitz-Jim redt punt voor Ajax tegen angstgegner AZ
items.summary: Ajax is er voor de elfde keer op rij niet in geslaagd om van AZ te winnen. De Amsterdammers sleepten in de slotfase nog een punt uit het vuur dankzij een treffer van Kian Fitz-Jim.

items.title: Samenvatting: PSV komt terug van achterstand tegen FC Groningen
items.summary: PSV ontsnapte zondag ternauwernood aan puntenverlies tegen FC Groningen. De Eindhovenaren keken na een ruim uur nog tegen een achterstand aan, maar Ismael Saibari en Dennis Man draaiden de wedstrijd om.

items.title: Samenvatting: Targhalline bezorgt Feyenoord zege in moeizame strijd met FC Utrecht
items.summary: Feyenoord boekte zondagmiddag een nipte en moeizame zege bij FC Utrecht. Oussama Targhalline werd met zijn goal in de eerste helft de matchwinner.

items.title: De Zerbi kondigt na recordnederlaag nieuw gesprek met bestuur aan
items.summary: Na de totale vernedering van Olympique Marseille bij Paris Saint-Germain heeft Roberto De Zerbi zijn excuses aangeboden aan zijn fans. De Italiaanse trainer heeft aangekondigd dat hij met het bestuur gaat praten om te kijken wat er moet gebeuren.

items.title: Harde conclusie voor Tengstedt bij Feyenoord: 'En dat geldt ook voor Steijn'
items.summary: Feyenoord kon zondag tegen FC Utrecht niet beschikken over Ayase Ueda, maar niet Casper Tengstedt of Sem Steijn was de vervanger van de spits in de basiself. Jakub Moder kreeg het vertrouwen van Robin van Persie en dat zorgde voor verbaasde gezichten bij de analytici.

items.title: European football: PSG thrash Marseille and return to summit of Ligue 1
items.summary: Dembélé doubles up in 5-0 mauling Kane and Díaz on target in Bayern win Ousmane Dembélé struck twice as Paris Saint-Germain blew away bitter rivals Marseille on Sunday, reclaiming top spot in Ligue 1 with a crushing 5-0 victory at the Parc des Princes. Dembélé opened the scoring after just 12 minutes and added a second before half-time as PSG delivered a real statement of intent going into the crucial months of the season. Continue reading...

items.title: Celtic to host Rangers in Scottish Cup quarter-finals
items.summary: Rangers host Old Firm rivals Celtic and holders Aberdeen face a potential trip to Dunfermline in the Scottish Cup quarter-finals.

items.title: Who has made Troy's Premier League team of the week?
items.summary: After every round of Premier League matches this season Troy Deeney gives us his team of the week. Do you agree with his choices?

items.title: 'The hope is there' - how Man City 'just' kept themselves in title race
items.summary: A generation of Manchester City supporters has not experienced a winning feeling at Anfield, but all that changed on a highly dramatic Sunday evening as they fought back for a thrilling 2-1 Premier League victory.

items.title: 'Killjoy' - did VAR get late drama in Liverpool v Man City right?
items.summary: Manchester City's win at Liverpool ended in complete chaos - but did the referee and the VAR get Dominik Szoboszlai's red card right?

items.title: 'I know who I am' - Chelsea boss Bompastor defiant after tough week
items.summary: Chelsea manager Sonia Bompastor has not had many tougher weeks in her managerial career and was defiant following their win at Tottenham.

items.title: City win sealed with a kiss after resilience of Guéhi twists title race | Andy Hunter
items.summary: Pep Guardiola cherished a first victory at Liverpool since Covid, earned by defining contributions from players who know what it takes Before joining his triumphant players to celebrate in front of Manchester City’s delirious away support, Pep Guardiola looked to the heavens above Anfield and blew a kiss. This stadium has tormented the City manager more often than most over the past decade but, should the title race twist as dramatically as this victory, his 11th and possibly final visit to Liverpool will be cherished as the turning point. Was Guardiola’s kiss one of thanks for Gianluigi Donnarumma, the goalkeeper who deflated Liverpool in the Champions League last season with Paris Saint‑Germain and denied them a 99th-minute equaliser with a stunning save from Alexis Mac Allister? Or for the nerveless precision of Erling Haaland, who had completed the visitors’ comeback from the penalty spot six minutes earlier? The resilience of Marc Guéhi and co in the face of Liverpool’s second-half recovery merited a smacker, too. The former Liverpool transfer target would eventually get a kiss from his manager, deservedly so. Continue reading...

items.title: 'A long way back' - Carra expects Liverpool to miss out on CL spots
items.summary: Jamie Carragher expects Liverpool to miss out on Champions League qualification through the Premier League.

items.title: Greaves prepares for PDC ProTour with perfect Women's Series weekend
items.summary: Beau Greaves kicked off the 2026 Women's Series with a perfect weekend in Germany as she made it 113 wins on the spin and 17 consecutive titles.
